panels.description: Worker FastAPI service responding to /metrics. If 0, the content pipeline is offline.
panels.title: Worker

panels.description: Postgres reachable via the local-brain-db datasource. If 0 the dashboard itself would fail — this is a belt+suspenders sanity check.
panels.title: Postgres

panels.description: nvidia-smi exporter. Missing = GPU not detected, degraded, or exporter down.
panels.title: GPU exporter

panels.description: Pyroscope profiling server. Non-critical (profiling is best-effort).
panels.title: Pyroscope

panels.description: Tempo traces backend.
panels.title: Tempo

panels.description: Uptime-kuma external health monitor.
panels.title: Uptime-kuma

panels.description: Tasks stuck in_progress longer than 30 minutes. Should be 0. Anything >0 means a stage hung — see docs/operations/troubleshooting.md.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Stuck tasks" FROM content_tasks WHERE status='in_progress' AND started_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 minutes';

panels.title: Stuck >30m

panels.description: Count of is_secret=true app_settings rows that are stored as PLAINTEXT (not encrypted at rest). Should be 0 — any row with is_secret=true should be encrypted with pgcrypto via plugins/secrets. Non-zero means a secret is stored in the clear and should be re-encrypted.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Plaintext secrets" FROM app_settings WHERE is_secret = true AND value != '' AND value NOT LIKE 'enc:v1:%';

panels.title: Plain secret

panels.description: Tasks rejected in the last 24h by a hard veto (citation_verifier / programmatic_validator / topic_delivery). Thresholds are calibrated to the autonomous topic-discovery baseline — expect 5-20/day during normal operation. Red only if the number suggests a regression (a new false-positive class is in play).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Hard rejects (24h)" FROM content_tasks WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours' AND status = 'rejected' AND (error_message ILIKE '%veto: programmatic_validator%' OR error_message ILIKE '%veto: citation_verifier%' OR error_message ILIKE '%veto: topic_delivery%');

panels.title: Hard rej 24h
Scheduled jobs — freshness + last-run status
panels.description: Auto-populated for every PluginScheduler job. Healthy = age below the job's expected interval (every-2h jobs sit at 0-7200s, every-60s jobs at 0-90s). Orange highlight = age suggests the loop is dead. ok=0 = ran recently but failed; click the job_name to grep Loki for details.

panels.title: Jobs whose last fire failed
panels.description: Number of scheduler jobs where the most recent fire status was 'err'. Healthy = 0. >0 = at least one job ran but raised — open the freshness table, sort by Last status, and check Loki for the underlying traceback.

panels.title: GT open
panels.description: Unresolved GlitchTip issues from the latest brain triage probe cycle. Sourced from audit_log details->>'issues_seen' on the most recent probe.glitchtip_triage_cycle row (5-min cadence). Triage at http://localhost:8080.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT (details->>'issues_seen')::int AS "Open issues"
FROM audit_log
WHERE event_type = 'probe.glitchtip_triage_cycle'
ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1;

panels.title: GT probe
panels.description: Seconds since the brain last ran the GlitchTip triage probe. Healthy < 600s (5-min cadence). >900s means the brain isn't iterating — check `docker logs poindexter-brain`.

panels.title: Sched depth
panels.description: Count of posts queued for future publish. Populated by `poindexter schedule batch ...` or `poindexter publish-at <id> <when>`. Zero is fine if the operator publishes manually; non-zero means the scheduled_publisher daemon will fire them at published_at.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Pending" FROM posts WHERE status = 'scheduled' AND published_at > NOW();

panels.title: Next slot in
panels.description: Seconds until the next scheduled publish fires. Decreases monotonically until the slot hits; then jumps to the next slot in the queue. NULL means the queue is empty.

targets.rawSql: SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MIN(published_at) - NOW()))::int AS "Seconds" FROM posts WHERE status = 'scheduled' AND published_at > NOW();

panels.title: Sched overdue
panels.description: Posts whose published_at has passed but status is still 'scheduled'. Should be 0 — the scheduled_publisher daemon polls every poll_interval_seconds (default 60s) and flips them to published. Anything >0 sustained for >2 minutes means the daemon is wedged or the worker is down; check `docker logs poindexter-worker | grep scheduled_publisher`.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Overdue" FROM posts WHERE status = 'scheduled' AND published_at <= NOW();

panels.title: Approved q
panels.description: Count of pipeline_tasks in 'approved' status — posts the operator has approved but not yet published. This is the post-approval, pre-publish queue (distinct from the awaiting_approval review queue). Once published, each gets media via the 4h backfill jobs. Zero means nothing is waiting. A sustained non-zero count is the cue to publish (`poindexter publish <id>` / go-live) — these will not publish themselves. See the 'Approved — awaiting publish' table below for the per-task detail.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS "Approved" FROM pipeline_tasks WHERE status = 'approved';
SELECT severity as metric, COUNT(*) as value FROM audit_log WHERE $__timeFilter("timestamp") GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
SELECT source as metric, COUNT(*) as value FROM audit_log WHERE $__timeFilter("timestamp") GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
SELECT event_type as metric, COUNT(*) as value FROM audit_log WHERE $__timeFilter("timestamp") GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
SELECT LEFT(topic, 50) as topic, status, quality_score, created_at::timestamp(0) FROM pipeline_tasks_view WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Upcoming scheduled publishes (next 24h)
panels.description: Posts queued to publish within the next 24 hours. Empty when the queue is dry. Pin to the dashboard playlist so operators can see at a glance what's coming up.

targets.rawSql: SELECT published_at AS "Publish at (UTC)", slug AS "Slug", title AS "Title", EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (published_at - NOW()))::int AS "In (s)" FROM posts WHERE status = 'scheduled' AND published_at BETWEEN NOW() AND NOW() + INTERVAL '24 hours' ORDER BY published_at LIMIT 25;

panels.title: Approved — awaiting publish
panels.description: Tasks the operator has approved (pipeline_tasks.status='approved') that have not yet been published. The post-approval, pre-publish queue — distinct from the awaiting_approval review queue surfaced elsewhere. Empty when nothing is waiting. Each row stays here until published (`poindexter publish <task>` / go-live), after which media generation runs via the 4h backfill jobs. Ordered most-recently-approved first.

targets.rawSql: SELECT left(task_id, 8) AS "Task", topic AS "Topic", updated_at AS "Approved at (UTC)" FROM pipeline_tasks WHERE status = 'approved' ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 25;

panels.title: Exporter Signals — LLM Engine · Embeddings · Schema · Brain · Prometheus

panels.description: poindexter_ollama_reachable — 1 if Ollama /api/tags returned 200 on the last scrape. Ollama is the local LLM engine the whole pipeline runs on, yet it had no health tile anywhere until now.
panels.title: Ollama reachable

panels.description: poindexter_ollama_model_count — number of models Ollama reports. 0 while reachable=1 is the 'up but no models pulled' failure the plain up-check misses — every generation would fail with that state.
panels.title: Ollama models loaded

panels.description: poindexter_embeddings_missing_posts — published posts with no embeddings row. Catches 'new posts stopped getting embedded' even when overall embedding throughput looks fine. 0 is healthy; any positive value means RAG/search coverage is silently degrading.
panels.title: Posts missing embeddings

panels.description: poindexter_unapplied_migrations_count — migration .py files on disk not yet recorded in schema_migrations. >0 means the running worker is on a stale schema (container updated, process not restarted). Surfaced by /api/health but never on a dashboard until now.
panels.title: Unapplied migrations

panels.description: Age of the most recent brain.cycle_heartbeat (time() - poindexter_brain_cycle_heartbeat_timestamp_seconds). The brain writes one heartbeat per ~5-min cycle; this is the delivery-plane dead-man's switch. 'No data' or red (>15m) means the brain has stopped — the same condition the BrainDeliveryDeadMansSwitch alert fires on, now visible on the dashboard.
panels.title: Brain heartbeat age
CPU Usage by Container
panels.description: Per-container CPU usage (cores) from cAdvisor. Each line is one container (name label).

panels.title: Memory Usage by Container
panels.description: Per-container RSS memory usage in bytes from cAdvisor. Each line is one container (name label).

panels.title: Container Restarts (24h)
panels.description: Number of container restarts in the last 24 hours from cAdvisor. Non-zero values warrant investigation.

panels.title: Recent brain decisions
panels.description: Cycle-complete heartbeats are filtered out (they were 100% of visible rows); this shows only substantive brain decisions.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  created_at,
  outcome,
  ROUND(confidence::numeric, 2) AS confidence,
  LEFT(decision, 100)           AS decision
FROM brain_decisions WHERE decision NOT LIKE 'Cycle complete%'
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 10

panels.title: Last successful restore test (h)
panels.description: Hours since probe.restore_test_completed last passed (#441 — a backup you can't restore is not a backup). The probe failed on 2026-06-30 with no dashboard surface; this closes that gap.

targets.rawSql: SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MAX(timestamp))) / 3600.0 AS "Hours since pass" FROM audit_log WHERE event_type = 'probe.restore_test_completed'

panels.title: Restore-test failures (7d)
panels.description: probe.restore_test_failed events in the last 7 days. Anything >0 needs a look at the dump chain before you need it for real.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS "Failures" FROM audit_log WHERE event_type = 'probe.restore_test_failed' AND timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

title: System Health
description: CHARTER: is every service up and are the queues draining — liveness, exporters, publish/approved queues, audit heartbeat. Answers 'is anything down or wedged?'. Merged system-health + infrastructure-data. Top section gives core service status + pipeline-level signals; bottom section is the full infrastructure detail (DB tables, hardware metrics, audit log, raw data explorer, system settings).
